user_message: Help me prep the partner engagement for Woodgrove Labs. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 17:17 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs partner engagement

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about partner engagement. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 17:50 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

Just checking in on the partner engagement proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Woodgrove Labs
Date: 2024-01-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into partner engagement.

[00:06:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:10:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:15:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:24:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:28:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-G trap (Misattributes attendee role); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-G failure mode (Misattributes attendee role)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-G -- Misattributes attendee role
